2605.24528.title: Hypothesis Generation and Inductive Inference in Children and Language Models
2605.24528.paper_summary: Real world decision-making requires constructing mental models under uncertainty over evidence, over the underlying causal rules, and over the state of the world itself. Which computational principles underpin human inference under such conditions, and do LLM-based agents exhibit similar behavior given matching constraints? We address these questions using an inductive inference Box Task in which participants, human children and LLM-based agents, infer a latent cause through sequential interaction with an uncertain environment. We formalize this task as program induction with Bayesian particle-based inference, admitting two complementary interpretations: (1) as a constraint satisfaction process over hypotheses, and (2) as a program synthesis problem in which hypotheses are executable programs evaluated against evidence. Using the constraint-based formulation, we show that children's behavior is best explained by a combination of subjective evidence reliability and online hypothesis generation, accounting for both their evidence-seeking patterns and their dissociation between task completion and rule generalization. Using the program synthesis formulation, we treat LLM-based agents as model organisms: controllable systems that allow systematic manipulation of task conditions. Across backends, LLM-based agents replicate children's responses to changes in evidence reliability and observability, including discounting unreliable evidence, seeking to resolve partial information, and dissociating between task completion and causal generalization. At the same time, LLM-based agents tend to over-observe and over-comply with instructions relative to children. These results suggest that while children and LLM-based agents adapt similarly to environmental structure, their information-seeking behavior exhibits distinct underlying costs and inductive biases.
2605.24528.paper_summary_zh: 現實世界的決策需要在不確定的證據、潛在的因果規則以及世界本身的狀態下構建心理模型。<lb/>在這樣的條件下，哪些計算原則支撐人類的推理，基於大型語言模型（LLM）的代理是否在相似的約束下展現出相似的行為？<lb/>我們使用一個歸納推理盒子任務來解決這些問題，在這個任務中，參與者，包括人類兒童和基於LLM的代理，通過與不確定環境的連續互動推斷潛在原因。<lb/>我們將這個任務形式化為基於貝葉斯粒子的程式歸納，接受兩種互補的解釋：(1) 作為對假設的約束滿足過程，和 (2) 作為假設是可執行程式的程式合成問題，這些程式根據證據進行評估。<lb/>使用基於約束的形式化，我們顯示兒童的行為最好是通過主觀證據可靠性和在線假設生成的結合來解釋，這解釋了他們的尋求證據模式以及他們在任務完成和規則概括之間的脫節。<lb/>使用程式合成的形式化，我們將基於LLM的代理視為模型生物：可控系統，允許系統性地操縱任務條件。<lb/>在不同的後端中，基於LLM的代理重現了兒童對證據可靠性和可觀察性的變化的反應，包括對不可靠證據的折扣、尋求解決部分信息，及在任務完成和因果概括之間的脫節。<lb/>同時，基於LLM的代理相較於兒童，往往過度觀察和過度遵循指示。<lb/>這些結果表明，雖然兒童和基於LLM的代理在適應環境結構方面相似，但他們的信息尋求行為顯示出不同的潛在成本和歸納偏見。
2605.24528.author: Jeffrey Qin et.al.
2605.24528.authors: Jeffrey Qin, Wasu Top Piriyakulki, Zhuangfei Gao, Mia Radovanovic, Jessica Sommerville, Kevin Ellis, Marta Kryven
